seoTitle: How to Record a Clip in ClickUp: Step-by-Step
metaDescription: Learn how to record, share, and manage screen recordings with ClickUp Clips, including audio capture, transcriptions, and turning clips into tasks.
intro: ClickUp's Clips feature lets you record your screen and share it as a link without leaving your workspace, cutting down on separate screen-recording tools. This walkthrough covers activating Clips, choosing what to capture, and using ClickUp Brain to transcribe the recording afterward. It's a quick way to explain something visually instead of writing it out.

steps.heading: Locate the Clip recorder
steps.body: Open your ClickUp workspace and find the camera icon in the quick action ribbon or inside a task's comment thread.

steps.heading: Start a recording and grant microphone access
steps.body: Click Record a Clip and allow ClickUp to access your microphone so audio is captured alongside the video.

steps.heading: Choose what to share
steps.body: Select whether to record your entire screen, a specific application window, or a single browser tab before starting.

steps.heading: Record, pause, or reset as needed
steps.body: Use the countdown to start, then manage the recording from the floating ribbon if you need to pause or restart.

steps.heading: Stop and review the clip
steps.body: Click the stop button to compile the recording, review it in the creation modal, and copy the shareable link.

steps.heading: Use ClickUp Brain for transcription
steps.body: Run the built-in AI transcription tool to generate a searchable text summary of the clip's content.

faq.question: Do I need a separate app to record clips in ClickUp?
faq.answer: No, Clips is a native feature, so once it's enabled you can record directly from the workspace without installing extra software.

faq.question: Can I turn a recorded clip into a task?
faq.answer: Yes, you can drop the finished clip into an existing task thread or generate a brand-new task from it.

faq.question: Does ClickUp transcribe my clips automatically?
faq.answer: Transcription runs through ClickUp Brain, which you trigger after recording to generate a searchable text summary.

faq.question: Can I capture just one browser tab instead of my whole screen?
faq.answer: Yes, when starting a recording you can choose between full screen, a specific app window, or a single browser tab.
